Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Afroman Wins Civil Trial over Use of Police Raid Footage in His Music Videos 143 upvotes, 17 comments
  2. OpenRocket 651 upvotes, 112 comments
  3. Show HN: I built 48 lightweight SVG backgrounds you can copy/paste 330 upvotes, 63 comments
  4. How many branches can your CPU predict? 40 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 147 upvotes, 29 comments
  6. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 315 upvotes, 77 comments
  7. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 412 upvotes, 112 comments
  8. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 233 upvotes, 60 comments
  9. Afroman found not liable in defamation case brought by Ohio cops who raided home 539 upvotes, 168 comments
  10. Pretraining Language Models via Neural Cellular Automata 30 upvotes, 6 comments
  11. OpenBSD: PF queues break the 4 Gbps barrier 32 upvotes, 7 comments
  12. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 181 upvotes, 68 comments
  13. Juggalo Makeup Blocks Facial Recognition Technology (2019) 61 upvotes, 18 comments
  14. The next fight over the use of facial recognition could be in the supermarkets 18 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. Astral to Join OpenAI 298 upvotes, 130 comments
  16. Consensus Board Game 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. 'Your Frustration Is the Product' 123 upvotes, 52 comments
  18. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 484 upvotes, 250 comments
  19. RX – a new random-access JSON alternative 124 upvotes, 60 comments
  20. OpenAI to Acquire Astral 112 upvotes, 55 comments
  21. LotusNotes 131 upvotes, 68 comments
  22. Warranty Void If Regenerated 437 upvotes, 263 comments
  23. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 56 upvotes, 25 comments
  24. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 169 upvotes, 96 comments
  25. Nvidia NemoClaw 344 upvotes, 225 comments
  26. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 55 upvotes, 31 comments
  27. Gluon: Explicit Performance 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 135 upvotes, 125 comments
  29. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 645 upvotes, 771 comments
  30. Iran war energy shock sparks global push to reduce fossil fuel dependence 163 upvotes, 183 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
